summary: `break` exits loop entirely. `continue` skips to next iteration. `pass` is a no-op placeholder.
oneLineSummary: break terminates loop; continue skips iteration; pass is a no-op statement.
keyTakeaways: continue does NOT skip loop-else block.

memoryTricks.trick: BREAK = Stop & Exit; CONTINUE = Next Item; PASS = Do Nothing.

commonErrors.fix: Use continue to skip remaining code in iteration

nextTopics.title: Functions & Scope
nextTopics.whyNext: Organize code into reusable modular functions.
